sections.paragraph:
θορυβάζω pres. pass. 2 sg. θορυβάζῃ (s. two next entries; Dositheus 71, 16; En 14:8; Etym. Mag. p. 633, 34; also Eus. Al. [MPG LXXXVI/1, 444c]) act. ‘cause trouble’; in our lit. only pass. w. intr. sense be troubled/distracted περί τι by or about someth. of a busy housewife Lk 10:41 (v.l. τυρβάζῃ, q.v.).—DELG s.v. θόρυβος.

pres. pres. = present

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

  1. sg. = singular

Dositheus Dositheus , gramm., noted for a bilingual work: Ars Grammatica, IV? a.d.—List 5

En En = I Enoch—List 2

Mag.

Mag. = De Magistratibus Populi Romani

mag. = magic(al)

Eus. Eus. = Eusebius of Caesarea, various works; IV a.d.—List 5

MPG MPG = Migne, Patrologia Graeca—List 5

act. act. = active

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

intr. intr. = intransitive

someth. someth. = something

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

q.v. q.v. = quod vide (which see, see that which has just been mentioned)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 458
